name: Galie 2009 PHIRST Tadalafil Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension Extract
population_or_context: Patients with idiopathic or associated pulmonary arterial hypertension, treatment-naive or on background bosentan.
sample_size: 405 randomized to placebo or tadalafil 2.5, 10, 20, or 40 mg once daily.
methods: Sixteen-week double-blind placebo-controlled dose-ranging PHIRST trial assessing 6-minute walk distance, WHO functional class, clinical worsening, health-related quality of life, and adverse events.
population_profile: Patients with pulmonary arterial hypertension; not healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 2.5, 10, 20, or 40 mg once daily for 16 weeks; 40 mg dose met the prespecified statistical threshold.
indication_context: Pulmonary arterial hypertension disease-treatment evidence; now an approved tadalafil indication, not healthy off-label prevention.
study_design: Multicenter randomized double-blind placebo-controlled dose-ranging trial.
safety_findings: Most common treatment-related adverse events with tadalafil were headache, myalgia, and flushing in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low for healthy men age 30 to 50; high only for PAH-specific pulmonary vascular disease context.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Disease-specific pulmonary vascular endpoint, high 40 mg daily dose, some background bosentan use, and limited transfer to systemic vascular prevention.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but indication and dose make transfer to healthy systemic cardiovascular health inappropriate without separate evidence.
